jd_text: We are looking for an experienced Enterprise Architect to define and lead the end-to-end architecture of a Sovereign Private Cloud environment on a 6-month contract. This role will be responsible for building a holistic architectural vision across infrastructure, platforms, and services, ensuring alignment with security, compliance, and national data sovereignty requirements. You will play a key role in shaping architecture strategy, producing high-quality documentation, and guiding multi-vendor delivery across a complex enterprise ecosystem. Key responsibilities Define and own the end-to-end enterprise architecture for a Sovereign Private Cloud environment. Develop and maintain architecture artefacts, including high-level designs, reference architectures, roadmaps, and standards. Translate business and regulatory requirements into scalable, secure, and compliant technical solutions. Lead architecture governance and ensure alignment with enterprise standards and frameworks (e.g., TOGAF). Collaborate with infrastructure, cloud, security, and application teams to ensure cohesive solution design. Provide architectural oversight across core platforms, including Red Hat OpenShift, Cisco networking, Dell infrastructure, and Pure Storage solutions. Evaluate and integrate multi-vendor technologies into a unified and interoperable architecture. Support program delivery teams by providing architectural guidance and resolving complex technical challenges. Ensure alignment with sovereign cloud requirements, including data residency, security controls, and compliance frameworks. Required experience and skills Proven experience as an Enterprise Architect or Lead Architect within large-scale infrastructure or cloud transformation programs. Strong background in private cloud architecture, including design and implementation of enterprise-grade environments. Deep understanding of Red Hat OpenShift and container platforms within hybrid or private cloud ecosystems. Solid experience with infrastructure technologies, including Cisco (networking), Dell (compute), and Pure Storage. Experience working within structured enterprise architecture frameworks such as TOGAF. Strong documentation skills, with the ability to produce clear and comprehensive architecture artifacts. Experience operating in complex, multi-vendor environments. Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including working with senior leadership and technical teams. Preferred qualifications TOGAF certification (or equivalent enterprise architecture certification). Experience with sovereign cloud or highly regulated environments (e.g., government, healthcare, financial services). Knowledge of security and compliance frameworks relevant to data sovereignty. Cloud certifications or exposure to hybrid cloud models. What success looks like A well-defined, scalable, and compliant sovereign private cloud architecture. High-quality architecture documentation enabling consistent delivery across teams and vendors. Strong alignment between business objectives, regulatory requirements, and technical implementation. Your consultant Michael Deane is a Senior Recruitment Consultant at Aspire Technology. He focuses on finding opportunities for C Suite, VP and senior talent, along with technical and Business Development professionals within healthcare technology, Defence and Enterprise infrastructure. across the Middle East. Michael has an extensive technical, leadership and recruitment background.
